title: Algorithmic Problems, Contracts, and Evidence · Structure Brief: Correctness Claim
summary: Turn vague routing and lookup requests into measurable input-output problems. Planned artifact: Add and verify the dsa correctness claim increment in an algorithm contract and adversarial case catalog. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: State partial and total correctness claims that can be challenged by counterexamples.
Specify inputs, outputs, preconditions, postconditions, and invalid cases before selecting an algorithm.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
